title: meriolix serrulata var. douglasii
intro: Meriolix serrulata var. douglasii, also known as the Douglas's fern, is a variety of the Meriolix serrulata plant, which is a type of evergreen fern native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The medicinal properties of Meriolix serrulata var. douglasii include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Meriolix serrulata var. douglasii can be made into a salve or tincture to be applied topically to the skin, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make teas and infusions that can be consumed orally.
